questions.question: The three basic fingerprint patterns recognized in dactyloscopy are:

choices.D: Whorl, radial loop, and plain arch

questions.explanation: The three fundamental fingerprint pattern types are: (1) Loop (most common, ~65%) — subdivided into radial and ulnar loops; (2) Whorl (~30%) — subdivided into plain, central pocket, double loop, and accidental; (3) Arch (~5%) — subdivided into plain and tented arch.

questions.question: The examination of handwriting to determine authorship falls under the discipline of:
questions.explanation: Questioned Document Examination (QDE) is a branch of forensic science that deals with the scientific examination of documents to determine authenticity, authorship, alterations, and origin. It includes handwriting analysis, typewriting examination, ink analysis, and paper examination.

questions.question: The chain of custody in criminal investigations primarily ensures:

choices.A: That investigators arrive at the crime scene quickly
choices.B: Continuous documentation of evidence handling to maintain its integrity and admissibility
choices.C: That suspects are kept in custody throughout the trial

questions.explanation: Chain of custody refers to the chronological documentation/paper trail showing the seizure, custody, control, transfer, analysis, and disposition of physical or electronic evidence. It ensures evidence is not tampered with and is admissible in court.

questions.question: Rigor mortis (post-mortem rigidity) typically begins how many hours after death?
questions.explanation: Rigor mortis (post-mortem stiffening due to ATP depletion and actin-myosin cross-linking) typically begins 2-6 hours after death, reaching maximum stiffness around 12 hours, then gradually dissipates over 24-48 hours. Temperature, activity before death, and environment affect timing.

questions.question: The primary analytical method used to identify and quantify drugs in blood or urine samples in forensic toxicology is:
questions.explanation: Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS) is the gold standard in forensic toxicology for identifying and quantifying drugs and their metabolites. It combines the separation capability of GC with the identification power of MS.

questions.question: In forensic ballistics, the striations on a bullet that link it to a specific firearm are produced by:

choices.A: The firing pin striking the cartridge
choices.B: The rifling (grooves and lands) inside the barrel
choices.C: The extractor removing the spent case
choices.D: The pressure of the propellant gases

questions.explanation: Rifling — spiral grooves cut into the interior of the barrel — imparts spin to the bullet for stability and creates unique microscopic striations (tool marks). These striations are like fingerprints of the firearm and can match bullets to specific weapons.

questions.question: DNA profiling for forensic identification typically examines:

choices.B: Short Tandem Repeats (STRs) at multiple loci

questions.explanation: Modern forensic DNA typing uses Short Tandem Repeat (STR) analysis at multiple genomic loci. STRs are highly polymorphic (vary greatly between individuals), making them excellent for identification. The Philippine National DNA Database uses a set of STR loci for identification.

questions.question: The systematic search method where the crime scene is divided into quadrants or zones is called:
questions.explanation: The zone/quadrant search method divides the crime scene into smaller sectors, with each sector searched separately. Other methods include: strip/lane (systematic back-and-forth), spiral (inward or outward), grid (double strip at right angles), and wheel/ray (from center outward).

questions.question: Livor mortis (post-mortem lividity) is the purplish discoloration of the skin caused by:

choices.B: Settlement and pooling of blood in dependent body parts after death
choices.C: Oxidation of hemoglobin at skin surface
choices.D: Bruising from physical trauma before death

questions.explanation: Livor mortis occurs when blood circulation stops, causing blood to pool by gravity into the lowest (dependent) body parts. It appears as purplish-reddish staining, typically beginning 1-2 hours after death, becoming fixed at 8-12 hours. Its location can indicate body position after death.

questions.question: The Questioned Document Examiner (QDE) characteristic called 'pen lift' refers to:

choices.A: The pen pressure applied while writing
choices.B: The momentary lifting of the writing instrument between strokes
choices.C: The angle at which the pen is held
choices.D: The speed at which writing is produced

questions.explanation: Pen lift refers to the momentary break when a writer lifts the writing instrument between letters or within a letter. The pattern of pen lifts, like other handwriting characteristics (pressure, slant, spacing, line quality), can help identify or eliminate a potential writer.
